title: My body has learned to relax here
subtitle: A present-tense affirmation for reclaiming bed as a safe, trusted place for rest.
excerpt: A short affirmation practice from Day 15 of the SLEEP program. Use to replace the nervous system's learned association between bed and wakefulness with a new one: bed as safe.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 15 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: An affirmation from Day 15 of the SLEEP program. The verb "learned" is deliberate: it frames relaxation in bed as an acquired skill rather than a natural state that your body has lost. Skills can be relearned. The nervous system is plastic. The affirmation speaks directly to that plasticity.
My body has learned to relax here.

Say it quietly in bed, hand on your chest. Three times. Let each repetition land lower: less in the thinking mind, more in the body. If you notice resistance (a feeling that this is not yet true), that's fine. You're giving the nervous system a new instruction, not making a declaration about the past.
Adapted from Day 15 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
